frameworks.name: frequency-tuned gain control
frameworks.one_line: recurrent tuned gain control shapes V1 contrast-dependent spatial-frequency tuning

frameworks.name: feed-forward linear filter pooling
frameworks.one_line: changes in feed-forward filter pooling as alternative mechanism for tuning shifts

frameworks.name: linear summation prediction
frameworks.one_line: linear prediction baseline against which sublinear cortical summation is contrasted

core_question: Are V1 contrast-dependent spatial-frequency tuning shifts inherited from LGN inputs or computed de novo in cortex?
key_assumptions: optimally-oriented gratings probe the relevant tuning axis
single-unit recordings in anesthetized macaque reflect cell mechanisms
manipulations: number of component gratings (3, 5, 7) in mixtures
key_findings: most V1 cells and magnocellular LGN cells summed sublinearly across frequencies
V1 summation was more linear at-or-above peak frequency than below it
LGN cells, including those with tuning shifts, showed little frequency-dependent summation
interpretations: V1 contrast-dependent spatial-frequency tuning is not inherited from LGN afferents
a frequency-tuned gain-control model captures the cortical summation pattern
